title: Static Delivery, HTTP Representations, URLs, Bases, and Origins · Transfer Build
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers static delivery, http representations, urls, bases, and origins into a changed versioned URL and delivery contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned URL and delivery contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.
Keep platform filesystem paths, POSIX publication paths, percent-encoded URL paths, queries, and fragments as distinct types.
Model root, project-subpath, preview, custom-domain, trailing-slash, and relative-link behavior under one explicit base URL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Static Request Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 8674 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Static Request Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace a browser request through url resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Static Request Model, keep Static Request Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-http-evidence-1uk1wy" that uses case 5519 to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Http Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Http Evidence, keep Http Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-http-evidence-1uk1wy
# Competency: ssg-http-evidence.
# Case 5519: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_http_evidence_1uk1wy(requests: int = 7, mapped_routes: int = 7, base_path: str = "/handbook/", broken_fragments: int = 0, media_types_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1uk1wy = "1uk1wy-yw1ku1-5519"
    assert evidence_variant_1uk1wy
    evidence_axes_1uk1wy = (
        "reader1uk1wy",
        "source5519",
        "parseyw1ku1",
        "route1uk1wy5519",
        "artifact55191uk1wy",
        "recoveryyw1ku15519",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1uk1wy)) == 6
    if requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1:
        return False, mapped_routes, "request-shape"
    if mapped_routes != requests:
        return False, mapped_routes, "route-mapping"
    if not base_path.startswith("/") or not base_path.endswith("/"):
        return False, mapped_routes, "base-path"
    if broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid:
        return False, broken_fragments, "delivery-contract"
    assert mapped_routes == requests
    return True, mapped_routes, "resolved-static-delivery"
